title: quoya cuneata
intro: Quoya cuneata, also known as the Quoya or Native Quoya, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Quoya cuneata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
